instruction: Use web-first assertions and user-visible locator patterns in Playwright tests
relevant_when: Agent writes Playwright test assertions or selects elements in test files
context: Playwright tests must use web-first assertions (expect(locator).toBeVisible(), .toHaveText(), .toHaveCount()) that auto-retry, not page.$() or page.textContent() which execute once. Locators must use getByRole, getByLabel, getByText, or getByTestId — not CSS selectors or XPath.

checklist.name: web-first-assertions
checklist.rule: Agent uses expect(locator).toBeVisible(), .toHaveText(), .toHaveCount(), .toBeEnabled(), .toContainText() instead of page.$(), page.textContent(), page.isVisible(), or page.evaluate() for element verification
checklist.relevant_when: Agent writes assertions in Playwright tests to check element state or content

checklist.name: user-visible-locators
checklist.rule: Agent uses getByRole, getByLabel, getByText, getByPlaceholder, or getByTestId instead of CSS selectors (page.locator('.class'), page.locator('#id')) or XPath
checklist.relevant_when: Agent selects elements in Playwright tests

checklist.name: getByRole-preferred
checklist.rule: Agent prefers getByRole with accessible name over getByText or getByTestId when the element has a clear ARIA role (buttons, headings, links, textboxes)
checklist.relevant_when: Agent writes locators for interactive elements like buttons, links, and form fields

checklist.name: no-page-dollar
checklist.rule: Agent does not use page.$(), page.$$(), or page.evaluate() to check element existence or content — these do not auto-retry and cause flaky tests
checklist.relevant_when: Agent writes Playwright tests that verify elements exist or have specific content
